title: 'Messiah without a mandate' and 'attack of the Argie ants'
description: Today's papers spotlight Andy Burnham on the eve of him becoming Britain's new prime minister.
content:
And now for a look at the Sunday papers.

title: Andrew Tate and Tristan Tate arrested in US after more UK charges laid
description: The Crown Prosecution Service says it has requested their extradition from the US, while their lawyer says they are "innocent".
content:
Controversial influencer Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan have been arrested in the US after British authorities announced they were bringing further charges against them.
The UK's Crown Prosecution Service (CPS) said Andrew Tate was being charged... [2066 chars]

title: The hidden cost of the night shift and how to sleep it off
description: More than three million people in the UK work night shifts, which can greatly impact their health. What can be done to help them?
content: It is four in the morning, and the ward is quiet. A junior doctor has been on her feet for nine hours. She is tired, her muscles are sore and her eyes are straining, but when her shift ends at six in the morning and she finally gets home, she struggl... [10136 chars]

title: Russians turn to cash putting more strain on slowing wartime economy
description: Russians have been hit by mobile internet shutdowns and more businesses are seeking to dodge tax after more than four years of war with Ukraine.
content:
Russians are returning to cash, as mobile internet shutdowns disrupt card payments, and more businesses seek to dodge tax under mounting financial pressure more than four years into the war with Ukraine.
Russia has added 1.56tn roubles (£14.8bn; $20b... [3081 chars]

title: Andrew and Tristan Tate arrested in Miami on UK charges of rape and sex trafficking
description: Britain to seek extradition over alleged sexual exploitation, ‘extreme pornography’, assault and indecent images of a child
content:
The social media influencers Andrew and Tristan Tate have been arrested in Miami on UK charges by federal authorities, the US Marshals Service has said.
